metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- 1 game board with 4 advancement tracks (Exploration, Science, Technology, Military) and a central territory map
- 16 civilization mats (each with a unique asymmetric ability)
- 5 income mats (one per player — tracks income across 5 eras)
- 5 capital city mats (each with a unique 9x9 grid for building placement)

#### Cards
- 43 tapestry cards — played at the start of each income turn for ongoing or immediate benefits
- 48 territory tiles — hexagonal tiles gained through the Exploration track, placed on the central map
- 33 tech cards — double-sided cards (basic/upgraded) gained from the Science track

#### Miniatures & Buildings
- 18 landmark miniatures — 3D plastic buildings gained from advancement tracks, placed on your capital city
- 65 income buildings (13 per player in 5 types) — wooden cubes placed on your income mat to expose income symbols

#### Tokens & Resources
- Resource tokens in 4 types: Food (green), Workers (orange/brown), Gold (yellow), Culture (purple/blue)
- Victory point tokens (1, 5, 10, 20 denominations)
- Player markers (one per player per track, plus score marker)
- Outpost tokens for the Military track

#### Other
- 1 rulebook and 1 reference guide
- 5 player reference cards
- First player token

subtopics.title: Initial Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Steps
1. Place the **game board** in the center with all 18 landmarks on their marked track spaces
2. Shuffle **territory tiles** face-down in a supply
3. Shuffle **tapestry cards** and **tech cards** into separate decks
4. Sort **income buildings** by type near the board
5. Deal each player: **2 civilization mats** (choose 1, return the other), 1 income mat, 1 capital city mat
6. Deal **3 tapestry cards** and **3 tech cards** to each player
7. Place player markers at the start of all 4 advancement tracks
8. All players simultaneously take their **first income turn** (Era 1 — gain starting resources from the leftmost income column)
9. Some civilizations modify setup or starting resources — resolve those now
10. Randomly determine first player

#### First Income Turn
All players simultaneously take their first income turn before the game begins proper. You gain resources from the leftmost exposed column on your income mat. This is your starting economy — use it wisely in Era 1.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Two Types of Turns
On your turn you do **one** of two things:

**1. Advance Turn** — Pay resources to move up one space on any advancement track:
- Pay the cost shown on the next space (varies by track and position — generally increases as you go higher)
- Gain the benefit of that space immediately
- If you reach a landmark space, take that miniature and place it on your capital city mat right away
- You may advance on any of the 4 tracks in any order across turns

**2. Income Turn** — When you cannot or choose not to advance:
- **Play a Tapestry Card** from your hand (optional but very important — gives bonuses for the upcoming era)
- **Activate your Civilization ability** (some trigger on income turns)
- **Gain income** from all exposed symbols on your income mat
- Your era advances by one — you're entering the next era
- You expose more income symbols by placing income buildings on your mat

#### Asynchronous Pacing
- Players take turns independently — you might take your 3rd income turn while others are still advancing in Era 2
- The game ends when ALL players have taken their **5th income turn**
- Players who finish early wait for others to complete their turns

#### Important Timing
- Tapestry cards are played BEFORE gaining income
- Landmarks must be placed on your capital city immediately when earned
- Income buildings must be placed on your income mat immediately when earned

subtopics.title: The Four Advancement Tracks
subtopics.content:
#### Exploration Track
- **Benefits**: Gain territory tiles to place on the central map, claim resources printed on tiles
- Explore face-down (random tile) or face-up (choose from available) depending on the space
- Place tiles adjacent to existing tiles on the map
- **Key landmarks**: Available at certain milestones on the track
- Territory tiles expand the shared map and can earn control bonuses

#### Science Track
- **Benefits**: Gain tech cards, upgrade tech cards for powerful permanent abilities
- Tech cards have two sides — basic and upgraded (flip when you hit upgrade spaces)
- Upgraded tech cards provide strong ongoing abilities or end-game VP
- **Key landmark**: Research Lab
- This track is best as a mid-game investment once you know what upgrades you need

#### Technology Track
- **Benefits**: Gain income buildings to place on your income mat (exposing income symbols)
- Income buildings come in 5 types matching the 5 income columns
- Each building placed exposes new income symbols for future income turns
- **Key landmark**: Tech Building
- This is the most important track for building your economic engine

#### Military Track
- **Benefits**: Place outposts on territories, conquer opponent territories, gain VP
- Conquering lets you topple opponent outposts on adjacent territories
- Military advances give direct VP and map control
- **Key landmark**: Armory
- Strongest in mid-to-late game when the map has many territories to contest

subtopics.title: Capital City & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Capital City Mat
- Your capital city is a 9-column grid where you place landmarks and income buildings
- Landmarks are 3D miniatures with specific shapes (1x1, L-shape, T-shape, etc.)
- Income buildings are simple 1x1 cubes that fill single spaces
- Pieces cannot overlap or extend outside the grid

#### City Scoring Rules
- Each **completed row** (all spaces filled) scores the VP value shown at that row's edge
- Each **completed column** (all spaces filled) scores the VP value shown at the column's top
- **Partially completed rows/columns score zero** — only fully completed lines count
- Completing rows AND columns is the key to high capital city scores

#### Placement Strategy
- Place landmarks first (they're bigger and harder to fit), then fill gaps with income buildings
- Each capital city mat has a different grid pattern — study yours during setup
- Some landmarks have awkward shapes — plan before placing or you'll block future rows/columns

subtopics.title: Endgame & Final Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### When Does the Game End?
The game ends when **all players** have taken their 5th and final income turn. Some players will finish before others.

#### Victory Point Sources
1. **VP tokens** earned during the game (from track spaces, tapestry cards, conquests, tech cards, civilization abilities)
2. **Capital city scoring** — Completed rows and columns on your capital city grid
3. **Upgraded tech card bonuses** — Some upgraded tech cards award end-game VP
4. **Tapestry card bonuses** — Some tapestry cards give VP based on final game state
5. **Territory control** — Points for controlled territories (civilization-dependent)

#### Winner
The player with the most VP wins. Ties broken by most remaining resources.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Principles

- **Focus on 2 tracks, not 4.** Spreading across all 4 advancement tracks dilutes your resources. Pick 2 tracks that synergize and push deep on them. The deeper you go, the better the rewards.

- **Technology track is king for beginners.** Income buildings from the Technology track are how you grow your economy. Prioritize getting income buildings early — they compound every era. More income = more resources = more advances.

- **Don't take income turns too early.** Every income turn means one fewer era of advancing. If you take income on turn 3 while others take it on turn 8, they'll have 5 more advances than you. Stretch your resources as far as possible before taking income.

- **Plan your capital city placement.** Before placing landmarks and income buildings, look at what shapes are coming. Try to complete full rows and columns — partial rows score zero. Place income buildings to fill gaps that landmarks can't reach.

- **Save strong tapestry cards for later eras.** A tapestry card that gives "2 VP per advance this era" is worth 4 VP if you advance twice, but 12 VP if you advance six times. Later eras with a bigger engine make tapestry cards much more valuable.

- **Gold is your emergency fund.** Don't spend gold frivolously — save it for when you're one resource short of a critical advance.

- **Read your civilization ability carefully.** Your civilization power shapes your entire strategy. If your civ rewards Exploration, lean into that track. If it rewards tech upgrades, push Science.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- Taking income too early (you lose potential advance turns)
- Ignoring the Technology track (income buildings are your economic engine)
- Placing landmarks poorly on the capital city grid (wasting rows/columns)
- Spreading evenly across all 4 tracks instead of focusing
- Not reading tapestry card timing — some are "this era" and some are "when played"
- Hoarding resources instead of advancing (resources don't score directly, advances do)
- Racing for landmarks someone else will reach first (pivot to a different track)

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Tapestry — A Guided Walkthrough

**Before You Start**
Everyone picks a civilization. For your first game, look for civilizations with straightforward abilities. Historians (gain VP for each advance) and Architects (extra capital city bonuses) are great starting picks. Avoid civilizations that completely change the rules like The Chosen or Inventors until you understand the base game.

**Era 1: The Setup Income Turn**
Everyone takes their first income turn simultaneously:
1. You won't play a tapestry card yet (save them for later eras when they're more impactful)
2. Gain income from the leftmost column of your income mat — this gives you your starting resources
3. Check your civilization ability — some give bonus resources or cards at setup

You should now have a handful of Food, Workers, Gold, and Culture, plus 3 tapestry cards and 3 tech cards in hand.

**Your First Advance Turns**
Now the real game begins. Going clockwise, each player takes one turn at a time.

Look at the bottom of each advancement track — the first few spaces are cheap (often costing just 1 resource). Here's a solid opening plan:

*Turn 1*: Advance on the **Technology track**. Pay the cost and gain an income building. Place that building on your income mat — you just unlocked better income for your next income turn!

*Turn 2*: Advance on your second focus track. **Exploration** is great for beginners — you gain territory tiles with free resources printed on them. Place the tile on the central map and collect the bonus.

*Turn 3*: Continue advancing on one of your two focus tracks. If a landmark is within reach (1-2 more spaces), consider pushing for it — landmarks are first-come, first-served!

**The Landmark Race**
When you reach a landmark space on a track, take that 3D building and place it on your capital city mat. Landmarks are big and oddly shaped, so plan where they'll go. Try to place them where they help complete rows and columns on your grid.

**When to Take Your Era 2 Income Turn**
When you're running low on resources and can't make meaningful advances, it's time for income. On your income turn:
1. Play a tapestry card if you have a good one for this era
2. Activate your civilization ability
3. Collect income from all exposed symbols — this should be better than Era 1 because of income buildings you placed!

**Eras 3-5: The Engine Grows**
Each era, your income should grow thanks to more income buildings. Use your growing resource base to advance further on your focus tracks. The sweet spot is 4-6 advances per era.

Late-game tapestry cards become huge. A card like "Gain 1 VP for each space you've advanced on any track" might be worth 15-30 VP in Era 5.

**The Final Income Turn**
Your 5th income turn ends your game. Score your capital city grid (completed rows and columns only), add up all VP tokens, and count any end-game bonuses from tech cards and tapestry cards.

**First Game Target Score**: Aim for 150-200 VP. Don't worry about winning — focus on understanding the advance-then-income rhythm and how income buildings power your engine.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Civilization Tier Guide

Tapestry's civs are famously unbalanced — Stonemaier released official adjustments after community data showed huge win-rate gaps.

**Tier S (Overpowered pre-nerf):** Futurists (79% win rate at 3p before nerfs), Isolationists (score for largest connected landmass — very strong with Exploration+Military).

**Tier A (Strong):** Historians (gain benefits when opponents gain landmarks — scales with player count), Craftsmen (30+ VP from civ mat scoring), Merrymakers (straightforward civ mat scoring).

**Tier B (Solid):** Militants (Military focus), Traders (resource flexibility, better at 3-5p), Inventors (extra tech upgrades), The Chosen (Science synergy).

**Tier C (Situational):** Mystics (high variance), Architects (strong at 2p, weak at 5p), Nomads (Exploration+Military required).

#### Track Pairings & Resource Costs

Each track costs a primary resource: Exploration=Food, Science=Workers, Technology=Gold, Military=Culture.

**Best pairings:** Exploration+Military (place tiles then conquer), Technology+Exploration (engine + resources), Science as mid-game accelerator to any 2-track base.

#### The Income Building Secret

**Critical:** Only the 1st, 3rd, and 4th income buildings per type generate future income. The 2nd is a dead placement unless you get the 3rd out before next income turn.

**#1 Skill:** Get 2-3 income buildings out BEFORE your first income turn. Each early building compounds ~3 extra resources over the game.

#### The Exploration Rush

Strongest known opening: Advance Exploration 4 times (territory tiles give resources, often net-zero cost), then Military twice (conquer for resources + 2nd income building). Gets 2 buildings out before first income turn.

#### Player Count
- **2P:** Both fill cities — city strategy dominant. Military weak.
- **3-4P:** Landmark racing real. Historians strong.
- **5P:** City less reliable. Military and territory control dominate.

#### Key Decisions
1. **Era 1:** Income buildings before first income turn (highest-impact decision)
2. **Era 2-3:** Commit to 2 focus tracks
3. **Era 3:** One more advance vs income — the pivotal decision
4. **Era 4:** Best tapestry card timing
5. **Era 5:** Closing capital city rows for 10+ VP

key_rulings.text: When you reach a landmark space on an advancement track, you take that landmark miniature. If another player has already taken it, you do not receive a landmark. Each landmark can only be claimed by one player.
On an income turn, you first play a tapestry card (optional), then activate your civilization ability, and then gain income. The tapestry card is played BEFORE you receive your income resources.

key_rulings.topic: Asynchronous pacing — players in different eras
key_rulings.text: Players do not need to be in the same era. One player may take their 3rd income turn while others are still advancing. The game ends only when ALL players have completed their 5th income turn.

key_rulings.topic: Income buildings must be placed immediately
key_rulings.text: When you gain an income building from an advancement track, you must place it on your income mat immediately. You cannot hold it in reserve.

key_rulings.topic: Capital city partial rows score zero
key_rulings.text: Only fully completed rows and columns on your capital city mat score victory points. A row or column with even one empty space scores zero points.

key_rulings.topic: Gold is not a wild resource
key_rulings.text: Gold is one of the four resource types (Food, Workers, Gold, Culture). It is not wild and cannot substitute for other resources unless a specific ability allows it.

key_rulings.topic: Landmarks must be placed on capital city immediately
key_rulings.text: When you gain a landmark from an advancement track, you must place it on your capital city mat immediately. Plan ahead — a poorly placed landmark can block future rows and columns.

key_rulings.topic: Conquer dice — choose result in later eras
key_rulings.text: When conquering via the Military track, you roll the conquer die. In the first era, you must take the rolled result. In later eras, you may choose between the rolled result and VP.
You can have at most 8 of each resource (coins, workers, food, and culture are resources). Any resources gained beyond 8 of a single type are lost. Victory points are NOT resources and have no cap.

key_rulings.topic: No actions after your final income turn
key_rulings.text: You may not respond to other players (e.g., tapestry cards) after your final income turn. Your active game is over. You may still passively gain VP from civilization abilities that trigger on other players' actions, but you cannot actively play cards or spend resources.

key_rulings.topic: Benefits and landmarks are mandatory; bonuses are optional
key_rulings.text: All bonuses in the game are optional. Benefits and landmarks are mandatory rewards. When you advance on a track, you must gain the benefit shown on the space (if possible) and must take any landmark earned. However, bonuses (the extra cost/reward shown below certain spaces) are always optional.

key_rulings.topic: Cannot activate same track space twice per turn
key_rulings.text: You may not activate the same space on an advancement track more than once during your turn. If civilization abilities or card effects push you back and allow you to advance again, you cannot re-activate a space already visited this turn.

key_rulings.topic: Conquer and topple — territory with 2 tokens is permanently unconquerable
key_rulings.text: When you conquer a territory controlled by an opponent, you place your outpost and topple the opponent's outpost. A territory with 2 tokens (in any combination of standing/toppled) cannot be conquered again by anyone. A toppled outpost still counts for adjacency purposes.

The income turn must be executed in order: 1) Activate civilization ability, 2) Play a tapestry card (skip on first income turn), 3) Upgrade one technology card (optional), 4) Score VP from exposed icons, 5) Gain income from exposed resource icons. Performing steps out of order is a common mistake.
